title: System and calibration, registration, and training methods
abstract: One variation of a method for manipulating a multi-link robotic arm includes: accessing a virtual model of the target object; extracting an object feature representing the target object from the virtual model; at the robotic arm, scanning a field of view of an optical sensor for the object feature, the optical sensor arranged on a distal end of the robotic arm proximal an end effector; in response to detecting the object feature in the field of view of the optical sensor, calculating a physical offset between the target object and the end effector based on a position of the object feature in the field of view of the optical sensor and a known offset between the optical sensor and the end effector; and driving a set of actuators in the robotic arm to reduce the physical offset.
claims:
Claims I claim: 1. A method for manipulating a multi-link robotic arm comprising: â¢ during a setup period: o unlocking joints in the robotic arm; o as a user manually moves the robotic arm in real space, recording a sequence of images through an optical sensor mounted to the arm and defining a field of view coincident an operating field of the robotic arm; o detecting an interaction pose at which an end effector on the robotic arm engages a template object; o based on a trajectory of the end effector proximal the interaction pose and changes in positions of features detected in the sequence of images, correlating a template feature detected in the sequence of images with the template object; and o defining the trajectory relative to the template feature in the field of view of the optical sensor based on positions of the template feature in the sequence of images; and â¢ during an operating period: o detecting a first feature analogous to the template feature in the field of view of the optical sensor; and o autonomously replicating the trajectory at the robotic arm by driving a set of actuators within the robotic arm based on positions of the first feature in the field of view of the optical sensor. 2. The method of Claim l: â¢ wherein detecting the interaction pose comprises detecting the interaction pose in response to selection of a manual trigger by the user; â¢ further comprising triggering the end effector to execute an interaction routine in response to selection of the manual trigger; â¢ wherein defining the trajectory comprises writing a prompt, to the trajectory, to execute the interaction routine in response to the robotic arm arriving at the interaction pose relative to the first feature; and â¢ wherein autonomously replicating the trajectory at the robotic arm comprises autonomously executing the interaction routine in response to the robotic arm 

 arriving at the interaction pose, defined relative to the first feature in the field of view of the optical sensor, during the operating period. 3. The method of Claim 2: â¢ wherein defining the trajectory comprises: o defining a first segment of the trajectory that precedes the interaction position and is located relative to the template feature based on positions of the template feature in images, in the sequence of images, recorded prior to selection of the manual trigger; and o defining a second segment of the trajectory that succeeds the interaction position and is located relative to a second feature in the field of view of the optical sensor based on positions of the second feature in images, in the sequence of images, recorded after selection of the manual trigger; â¢ wherein autonomously replicating the trajectory at the robotic arm comprises: o over a first period of time, autonomously driving the set of actuators to replicate the first segment of the trajectory based on positions of the first feature in the field of view of the optical sensor; o during execution of the interaction routine over a second period of time succeeding the first period of time, autonomously driving the set of actuators to replicate the second segment of the trajectory based on positions of the secondary feature, analogous to the second template feature, in the field of view of the optical sensor. 4. The method of Claim 1, wherein detecting the interaction pose comprises: â¢ recording a sequence of joint postures of joints in the robotic arm in a machine reference frame during the setup period; â¢ detecting a directional reversal of the end effector in real space in the sequence of joint postures; and â¢ defining the interaction pose relative to the template feature based on a position of the template feature in a particular image, in the sequence of images, recorded nearest a time of the directional reversal of the end effector. 5. The method of Claim 1: â¢ wherein recording the sequence of images comprises: 

 o recording an image at each keypoint in a sequence of keypoints along a path traversed by the end effector during the setup period; and o recording a sequence of joint postures of joints in the robotic arm in machine coordinates at each keypoint in the sequence of keypoints; wherein defining the trajectory relative to the template feature comprises: o defining the trajectory of the end effector relative to the template feature in the field of view of the optical sensor based on positions of the template feature in the sequence of images and a known offset between the optical sensor and the end effector; and o defining a sequence of target joint postures of joints in the robotic arm along the trajectory based on the sequence of joint postures; wherein autonomously replicating the trajectory at the robotic arm comprises: o driving the set of actuators to approximate the sequence of target joint postures; and o deviating from the sequence of target joint postures based on positions of the first feature in the field of view of the optical sensor to move the end effector along the trajectory relative to the first feature. The method of Claim 1: wherein recording the sequence of images comprises: o recording a pre-interaction image prior to the robotic arm occupying the interaction pose; and o recording a post-interaction image once the robotic arm leaves the interaction pose; further comprising detecting a first difference between the pre-interaction image and the post-interaction image proximal the template feature; and further comprising, during the operating period: o recording a first image prior to the robotic arm approximating the interaction pose relative to the first feature; o recording a second image once the robotic arm leaves the interaction pose; and o confirming completion of the trajectory in response to a second difference between the first image and the second image proximal the first features approximating the first difference. 7. The method of Claim 1: â¢ wherein correlating the template feature with the template object comprises detecting a constellation of template features present and increasing in scale in a subset of the sequence of images as the robotic arm approaches the interaction pose; â¢ wherein defining the trajectory comprises defining the trajectory relative to the constellation of template features in the field of view of the optical sensor based on positions of the constellation of template features in the subset of images; â¢ wherein detecting the first feature during the operating period comprises scanning the field of view of the optical sensor for a first constellation of features analogous to the constellation of template features; and â¢ wherein autonomously replicating the trajectory at the robotic arm comprises driving the set of actuators within the robotic arm based on positions and orientations of the first constellation of features in the field of view of the optical sensor. 8. The method of Claim 1: â¢ wherein defining the trajectory comprises: o detecting the template feature in a first image in the sequence of images; o determining that the template feature is absent in a second image, in the sequence of images, recorded after the first image and before the end effector enters the interaction pose; o detecting a second template feature present in the first image and in the second image; o defining a first segment of the trajectory, preceding the interaction pose, relative to the template feature; and o defining a second segment of the trajectory, succeeding the first segment of the trajectory and containing the interaction pose, relative to the second template feature; and â¢ wherein autonomously replicating the trajectory comprises: o driving the set of actuators to replicate the first segment of the trajectory based on positions of the first feature detected in the field of view of the optical sensor; and o in response to completing the first segment of the trajectory, driving the set of actuators to replicate the second segment of the trajectory based on positions 

 of a second feature, analogous to the second template feature, detected in the field of view of the optical sensor. The method of Claim 8: wherein defining the first segment of the trajectory comprises defining the first segment of the trajectory relative to the template feature that represents an interaction surface on the template object; wherein defining the second segment of the trajectory comprises defining the second segment of the trajectory relative to the second template feature that represents an edge of the template object and is offset from the interaction surface on the template object; wherein detecting the first feature analogous to the template feature during the operating period comprises detecting the first feature analogous to the template feature and detecting a second feature analogous to the second template feature in the field of view of the optical sensor, the first feature and the second feature representing a target object analogous to the template object; wherein driving the set of actuators to replicate the second segment of the trajectory comprises driving the set of actuators to locate the second template feature in a position in the field of view of the optical sensor associated with the interaction pose, wherein an interface surface on the end effector contacts a region of the target object represented by the first feature when the end effector occupies the interaction pose. The method of Claim 1: further comprising: o in a first subset of images in the sequence of images, detecting the template feature at a first resolution less than a threshold resolution, the template feature representing the template object; o in a second subset of images succeeding the first subset images in the sequence of images, detecting the template feature at a second resolution greater than the threshold resolution; o in the first subset of images, detecting a global reference feature at a resolution greater than the threshold resolution, the global reference feature proximal the template feature and distinct from the target object; wherein defining the trajectory comprises 

 o defining a first segment of the trajectory relative to the global reference feature; and o defining a second segment of the trajectory, succeeding the first segment of the trajectory and containing the interaction pose, relative to the template feature; and wherein autonomously replicating the trajectory comprises: o driving the set of actuators to replicate the first segment of the trajectory based on positions of a second feature analogous to the global reference feature detected in the field of view of the optical sensor; and o in response to completing the first segment of the trajectory, driving the set of actuators to replicate the second segment of the trajectory based on positions of the first feature detected in the field of view of the optical sensor. . A method for manipulating a multi-link robotic arm comprising: accessing a virtual model of the target object; extracting an object feature representing the target object from the virtual model; at the robotic arm: o scanning a field of view of an optical sensor for the object feature, the optical sensor arranged on a distal end of the robotic arm proximal an end effector; o in response to detecting the object feature in the field of view of the optical sensor, calculating a physical offset between the target object and the end effector based on a position of the object feature in the field of view of the optical sensor and a known offset between the optical sensor and the end effector; and o driving a set of actuators in the robotic arm to reduce the physical offset. !. The method of Claim 11: wherein extracting the object feature from the virtual model comprises extracting a three-dimensional constellation of features representing the target object from the virtual model defining a three-dimensional representation of the target object, the three-dimensional constellation of features comprising the object feature; wherein scanning the field of view for the object feature comprises: o recording a first image through the optical sensor; and o extracting a set of features from the first image; wherein calculating the physical offset comprises 

 o confirming a match between a subset of the set of features and a subset of the three-dimensional constellation of features; and o calculating a linear offset and a rotational offset from the end effector to the target object based on a position and an orientation of the subset of features in the first image; and â¢ wherein driving the set of actuators to reduce the physical offset comprises driving actuators in multiple joints in the robotic arm to reduce the linear offset and the rotational offset between the end effector and the target object. 13. The method of Claim 12: â¢ wherein accessing a virtual model of the target object comprises accessing a virtual three-dimensional solid model of the target object; â¢ wherein extracting the three-dimensional constellation of features from the virtual model comprises extracting a set of edge features and vertex features from multiple sides of the virtual model; and â¢ wherein scanning the field of view for the object feature comprises scanning the first image for a cluster of features defining relative sizes, geometries, and positions approximating a subset of the set of edge features and vertex features. 14. The method of Claim 11: â¢ further comprising receiving an indicator of an interaction surface on the virtual model; â¢ wherein extracting the object feature from the virtual model comprises extracting the object feature representing the interaction surface from the virtual model; â¢ wherein calculating the physical offset between the target object and the end effector comprises calculating the physical offset between the interaction surface and an interface surface on the end effector based on the position of the object feature in the field of view of the optical sensor and a known offset between the optical sensor and the interface surface on the end effector; and â¢ wherein driving the set of actuators to reduce the physical offset comprises driving the set of actuators to move the interface surface on the end effector into contact with the interaction surface on the target object based on the physical offset. 15. The method of Claim 11: 

â¢ further comprising retrieving a preplanned trajectory of an interaction routine, the preplanned trajectory defined relative to a template feature; â¢ wherein scanning the field of view of the optical sensor for the object feature comprises scanning the field of view of the optical sensor for the object feature analogous to the template feature; â¢ further comprising virtually aligning the template feature to the object feature detected in the field of view of the optical sensor to locate the preplanned trajectory relative to the object feature, the preplanned trajectory extending from an interaction pose to a release pose offset from and succeeding the interaction pose, the end effector contacting the target object between the interaction pose and the release pose; â¢ wherein driving the set of actuators to reduce the physical offset comprises: o driving the set of actuators to move the end effector from a current pose to the interaction pose; and o in response to entering the interaction pose, driving the set of actuators to move the end effector from the interaction pose to the release pose along the preplanned trajectory located relative to the object feature. 16. The method of Claim 15, â¢ wherein retrieving the preplanned trajectory for the interaction routine comprises: o receiving selection of the interaction routine from a set of predefined interaction routines; o retrieving the preplanned trajectory that defines: â an approach direction, approach speed, and approach orientation of the end effector between the interaction pose and the release pose relative to the template feature; and â an action executable by the end effector at the release pose; â¢ wherein driving the set of actuators to reduce the physical offset further comprises triggering the end effector to execute the action in response to entering the release pose. 17. The method of Claim 16, wherein driving the set of actuators to move the end effector from the current pose to the interaction pose comprises: â¢ calculating a shortest viable trajectory from the current pose to the interaction pose relative to the object feature detected in the field of view of the optical sensor; and 

 driving the set of actuators to move the end effector from the current pose to interaction pose along the shortest viable trajectory. 18. The method of Claim 11, further comprising: â¢ accessing a second virtual model of a second target object; â¢ extracting a second object feature representing the second target object from the second virtual model; â¢ at the robotic arm: o scanning a field of view of the optical sensor for the second object feature; o in response to detecting the second object feature in the field of view of the optical sensor, calculating a second physical offset between the second target object and the end effector based on a second position of the second object feature in the field of view of the optical sensor and the known offset between the optical sensor and the end effector; and o in response to the end effector contacting the target object at a release pose: â driving the set of actuators to retract the end effector from the release pose; and â driving the set of actuators to reduce the second physical offset. 19. The method of Claim 18: â¢ wherein driving the set of actuators to reduce the physical offset comprises: o driving the set of actuators to move the end effector into contact with the target object at the release pose; and o in response to the end effector occupying the release pose, triggering the end effector to grasp the target object; â¢ wherein driving the set of actuators to reduce the second physical offset comprises driving the set of actuators to move the end effector and the target object toward the second target object in response to the end effector grasping the target object; and â¢ further comprising, in response to the end effector occupying a second release pose proximal the second target object, triggering the end effector to release the target object. 20. The method of Claim 11: 

 wherein accessing the virtual model of the target object comprises accessing a pre- action virtual model of the target object and a post-action virtual model of the target object; and wherein extracting the object feature representing the target object from the virtual model comprises: o detecting a difference between the pre-action virtual model of the target object and the post-action virtual model of the target object; and o extracting the object feature from a region of the pre-action virtual model of the target object coinciding with the difference. .. A method for manipulating a multi-link robotic arm, the method comprising: at a first time, recording a first optical image through an optical sensor arranged proximal a distal end of the robotic arm proximal an end effector; detecting a global reference feature in a first position in the first optical image; in response to detecting the global reference feature in the first optical image, virtually locating a preplanned trajectory relative to the first position of the global reference feature in the first optical image, the preplanned trajectory defining an object keypoint representing an estimated location of a target object within range of the end effector; driving a set of actuators within the robotic arm to move the end effector along the preplanned trajectory, virtually located relative to the global reference feature, toward the object keypoint; at a second time succeeding the first time, recording a second optical image through the optical sensor; detecting an object feature in a second position in the second optical image, the object feature representing the target object; in response to detecting the object feature in the second optical image, virtually aligning the object keypoint of the preplanned trajectory to the object feature based on the second position of the object feature in the second optical image; and driving the set of actuators to move the end effector along the preplanned trajectory, virtually aligned to the object feature, toward the target object. i.The method of Claim 21: further comprising, prior to the first time: 

 o recording a sequence of keypoint images during manual manipulation of the end effector from a first pose to a second pose in real space during a setup routine; and o extracting positions of the global reference feature and positions of a template object feature from the sequence of keypoint images; and o compiling positions of the global reference feature and the object feature in the sequence of keypoint images into the preplanned trajectory comprising a first keypoint defining the first pose relative to the global reference feature and terminating at the object keypoint defining the second pose relative to the object feature; â¢ wherein virtually locating the preplanned trajectory relative to the first position of the global reference feature in the first optical image comprises calculating a first offset between the first position of the global reference feature in the first optical image and a second keypoint position of the global reference feature in a first keypoint image in the preplanned trajectory; â¢ wherein driving the set of actuators to move the end effector along the preplanned trajectory comprises driving the set of actuators to reduce the first offset; â¢ wherein virtually aligning the object keypoint of the preplanned trajectory to the object feature comprises calculating a second offset between the second position of the object feature in the second optical image and a position of the template feature in a second keypoint image in the preplanned trajectory; and â¢ wherein driving the set of actuators to move the end effector along the preplanned trajectory, virtually aligned to the object feature, comprises driving the set of actuators to reduce the second offset. 23. The method of Claim 22: â¢ wherein virtually locating the preplanned trajectory relative to the first position of the global reference feature in the first optical image comprises calculating a transform that maps the global reference feature in the first optical image to the global reference feature in the first keypoint image; and â¢ wherein driving the set of actuators to reduce the first offset comprises driving the set of actuators as a function of the transform based on a stored motion model of the robotic arm. 24. The method of Claim 21: 

â¢ wherein virtually aligning the object keypoint of the preplanned trajectory to the object feature comprises: o calculating a position of the object feature relative to the robotic arm based on the second position, a skew, and a size of the object feature in the second optical image; o aligning the object keypoint of the preplanned trajectory to the position of the object feature; o redefining the preplanned trajectory from a keypoint in the preplanned trajectory adjacent the second pose of the end effector to the object keypoint aligned to the object feature; and â¢ wherein driving the set of actuators to move the end effector along the preplanned trajectory, virtually aligned to the object feature, toward the target object comprises: o driving the set of actuators to move the end effector onto the preplanned trajectory virtually aligned to the object feature; and o in response to the end effector coinciding with the preplanned trajectory, driving the set of actuators to move the end effector along the preplanned trajectory toward the target object. 25. The method of Claim 21: â¢ wherein detecting the global reference feature in the first optical image comprises extracting the first position, a first skew, a first orientation, and a first size of the global reference feature from the first optical image; â¢ wherein virtually locating the preplanned trajectory relative to the first position of the global reference feature in the first optical image comprises: o virtually locating an origin of a global reference frame based on the first position of the global reference feature; o aligning axes of the global reference frame within real space to the global reference feature based on the first skew, the first orientation, and the first size of the global reference feature in the first optical image; and o accessing the preplanned trajectory defined in the global reference frame; â¢ wherein detecting the object feature in the second position in the second optical image comprises extracting the second position, a second skew, a second orientation, and a second size of the object feature from the second optical image; â¢ wherein virtually aligning the object keypoint of the preplanned trajectory to the object feature comprises: 

 o virtually locating an origin of an object reference frame based on the second position of the object feature; o aligning axes of the object reference frame within real space to the object reference feature based on the second skew, the second orientation, and the second size of the object reference feature in the second optical image; and o projecting the preplanned trajectory into the object reference frame with the object keypoint of the preplanned trajectory located relative to the object feature. 26. The method of Claim 25: â¢ wherein driving the set of actuators within the robotic arm to move the end effector along the preplanned trajectory, virtually located relative to the global reference feature, toward the object keypoint comprises: o calculating a first pose of the end effector within the global reference frame based on the first position, the first skew, the first orientation, and the first size of the global reference feature in the first optical image; o calculating a first offset between the first pose and the preplanned trajectory in the global reference frame; and o driving the set of actuators within the robotic arm to reduce the first offset; and â¢ wherein driving the set of actuators to move the end effector along the preplanned trajectory, virtually aligned to the object feature, toward the target object comprises: o calculating a second pose of the end effector within the object reference frame based on the second position, the second skew, the second orientation, and the second size of the object reference feature in the second optical image; o calculating a second offset between the second pose and the preplanned trajectory in the object reference frame; and o driving the set of actuators within the robotic arm to reduce the second offset. 27. The method of Claim 21: â¢ wherein detecting the global reference feature in the first optical image comprises, at approximately the first time: o extracting a first constellation of features from the first optical image; o calculating a first confidence that the first constellation of features represents the global reference feature; 

 o calculating a second confidence that the first constellation represents the object feature; o detecting presence of the global reference feature in a field of view of the optical sensor at approximately the first time in response to the first confidence exceeding a threshold value; and o detecting absence of the object feature in the field of view of the optical sensor at approximately the first time in response to the threshold value exceeding the second confidence; â¢ wherein virtually locating the preplanned trajectory relative to the global reference feature comprises virtually aligning the preplanned trajectory relative to the global reference feature in response to detecting presence of the global reference feature and in response to detecting absence of the object feature in the field of view of the optical sensor; â¢ wherein detecting the global reference feature in the first optical image comprises, at approximately the second time: o extracting a second constellation of features from the second optical image; o calculating a fourth confidence that the second constellation represents the object feature; and o detecting presence of the object feature in the field of view of the optical sensor at approximately the second time in response to the fourth confidence exceeding the threshold value; and â¢ wherein virtually aligning the object keypoint of the preplanned trajectory to the object feature comprises virtually aligning the object keypoint of the preplanned trajectory to the object feature in response to detecting presence of the object feature in the field of view of the optical sensor. 28. The method of Claim 27: â¢ further comprising: o at an intermediate time succeeding the first time and preceding the second time, recording an intermediate optical image through the optical sensor; o at approximately the intermediate time: â extracting an intermediate constellation of features from the intermediate image; â calculating a fifth confidence that the intermediate constellation of features represents the global reference feature; â calculating a sixth confidence that the intermediate constellation represents the object feature; â detecting presence of the global reference feature in the field of view of the optical sensor at approximately the intermediate time in response to the fifth confidence exceeding the threshold value; and â detecting presence of the object feature in the field of view of the optical sensor at approximately the intermediate time in response to the sixth confidence exceeding the threshold value; and â virtually locating the preplanned trajectory relative to the global reference feature in response to detecting presence of the global reference feature and in response to a resolution of the object feature in the field of view of the optical sensor remaining below a threshold resolution; and â¢ wherein virtually aligning the object keypoint of the preplanned trajectory to the object feature comprises virtually aligning the object keypoint of the preplanned trajectory to the object feature in response to detecting presence of the object feature in the field of view of the optical sensor and in response to a resolution of the object feature in the field of view of the optical sensor exceeding the threshold resolution. 29. The method of Claim 27, further comprising: â¢ in response to detecting presence of the global reference feature in the field of view of the optical sensor at approximately the first time, calculating a position of a global reference frame relative to the robotic arm at approximately the first time based on the first position of the global reference feature in the first optical image; â¢ calculating a third confidence that the second constellation of features represents the global reference feature; â¢ detecting presence of the global reference feature in the field of view of the optical sensor at approximately the second time in response to the third confidence exceeding the threshold value; and â¢ in response to detecting presence of the global reference feature in the field of view of the optical sensor at approximately the second time, recalculating the position of the global reference frame relative to the robotic arm at approximately the second time based on the second position of the global reference feature in the second optical image. 30. The method of Claim 21, â¢ wherein detecting the global reference feature in the first position in the first optical image comprises detecting the global reference feature comprising a predefined reference fiducial arranged in a fixed position relative to a base of the robotic arm; â¢ further comprising: o accessing a virtual model of the target object; o extracting a unique constellation of features from the virtual model; and o associating the unique constellation of features with the target object; and â¢ wherein detecting the object feature in the second position in the second optical image comprises: o extracting a set of features from the second optical image; and o matching the set of features to the unique constellation of features to identify the target object in the second optical image. 31. The method of Claim 30: â¢ wherein detecting the global reference feature in the first position in the first optical image comprises detecting the predefined reference fiducial arranged on a fastener dispenser containing a set of threaded fasteners, the set of threaded fasteners comprising the target object; and â¢ wherein detecting the object feature in the second position in the second optical image comprises matching the set of features to the unique constellation of features representing a screw head to identify the target object in the second optical image. 32. The method of Claim 21, further comprising: â¢ at a third time succeeding the second time, driving the set of actuators to move the end effector into contact with the target object; â¢ triggering the end effector to engage the target object; â¢ virtually locating a second preplanned trajectory relative to the global reference feature, the second preplanned trajectory extending from the object keypoint to a target keypoint approximating a release position for the target object; and â¢ at a fourth time succeeding the third time, driving the set of actuators to move the end effector and the target object along the second preplanned trajectory away from the object keypoint and toward the target keypoint, the second preplanned trajectory virtually located relative to the global reference feature. 33. The method of Claim 32: â¢ further comprising: o at approximately the fourth time, recording a fourth optical image through the optical sensor; o detecting absence of the global reference feature and absence of a target feature in the fourth optical image, the target feature representing the release position for the target object; â¢ wherein driving the set of actuators to move the end effector and the target object along the second preplanned trajectory comprises, at the fourth time, implementing closed-loop controls to retract the end effector along the second preplanned trajectory based on known lengths of segments of the robotic arm and outputs of angular position sensors arranged in joints between segments of the robotic arm in response to detecting absence of the global reference feature and absence of the target feature in the fourth optical image; â¢ further comprising: o at a fifth time succeeding the fourth time, recording a fifth optical image through the optical sensor; o detecting the global reference feature in a fifth position in the fifth optical image; o in response to detecting the global reference feature in the fifth optical image, virtually locating the second preplanned trajectory relative to the fifth position of the global reference feature in the fifth optical image; and o driving the set of actuators to move the end effector along the second preplanned trajectory, virtually located relative to the fifth position of the global reference feature in the fifth optical image, toward the target keypoint. 34. The method of Claim 32: â¢ further comprising: o at a sixth time succeeding the fourth time, recording a sixth optical image through the optical sensor; o detecting a target feature in a sixth position in the sixth optical image, the target feature representing the release position for the target object; o in response to detecting the target feature in the sixth optical image, virtually aligning the target keypoint of the second preplanned trajectory to the target 

 feature based on the sixth position of the target feature in the sixth optical image; and o driving the set of actuators to move the end effector along the second preplanned trajectory, virtually aligned to the target feature, toward the release position. 35. The method of Claim 34, further comprising: â¢ at a seventh time succeeding the sixth time, driving the set of actuators to move the end effector into the release position to contact the target object against a surface, the target object comprising a threaded fastener, and the release position coinciding with a bore; â¢ in response to the target object entering the release position: o rotating the end effector; and o driving the set of actuators to advance the end effector forward in a direction parallel to an axis of the target object and at a rate proportional to a rotational speed of the end effector; and â¢ in response to a torque on the end effector exceeding a threshold torque, driving the set of actuators to retract the end effector from the release position parallel to the axis of the target object. 36. The method of Claim 32: â¢ further comprising: o at an intermediate time succeeding the second time and preceding the third time, recording an intermediate optical image through the optical sensor; o detecting the object feature in an intermediate position in the intermediate optical image; o in response to detecting the intermediate object feature in the intermediate optical image: â characterizing a change in pose of the end effector in real space from the second time to the intermediate time based on a difference between the second position of the object feature in the second optical image and the intermediate position of the object feature in the intermediate image; â characterizing a sequence of outputs of angular position sensors in joints of the robotic arm from the second time to the intermediate time; â calibrating a motion model for the robotic arm based on the change in pose of the end effector in real space and the sequence of outputs of angular position sensors; â¢ wherein driving the set of actuators to move the end effector into contact with the target object comprises: o at approximately the third time, recording a third optical image through the optical sensor; o detecting absence of the global reference feature and absence of the object feature in the third optical image; o in response to detecting absence of the global reference feature and absence of the object feature in the third optical image, driving the set of actuators to move the end effector into contact with the target object based on the motion model and outputs of the angular position sensors. 37. A method for manipulating a multi-link robotic arm, the method comprising: â¢ at a first time, recording a first optical image through an optical sensor arranged proximal a distal end of the robotic arm proximal an end effector; â¢ detecting a global reference feature in a first position in the first optical image; â¢ virtually locating a global reference frame based on the first position of the global reference feature in the first optical image; â¢ calculating a first pose of the end effector within the global reference frame at approximately the first time based on the first position of the global reference feature in the first optical image; â¢ driving a set of actuators within the robotic arm to move the end effector from the first pose toward an object keypoint, the object keypoint defined within the global reference frame and representing an estimated location of a target object within range of the end effector; â¢ at a second time succeeding the first time, recording a second optical image through the optical sensor; â¢ detecting an object feature in a second position in the second optical image, the object feature representing the target object; 

â¢ calculating a second pose of the end effector relative to the target object at approximately the first time based on the second position of the object feature in the second optical image; and â¢ driving the set of actuators to move the end effector from the second pose toward the target object. 38. The method of Claim 37: â¢ wherein virtually locating the global reference frame comprises virtually locating a preplanned trajectory based on the first position of the global reference feature in the first optical image, the preplanned trajectory defining a three-dimensional target path for the end effector within the global reference frame and terminating at the object keypoint defined within the global reference frame; and â¢ wherein driving the set of actuators to move the end effector from the first pose toward the object keypoint comprises driving the set of actuators to move the end effector from the first pose onto the preplanned trajectory and along the preplanned trajectory toward the object keypoint in the global reference frame; â¢ further comprising, in response to detecting the object feature in the second optical image: o virtually locating an object reference frame based on the second position of the object feature in the second optical image; and o projecting the preplanned trajectory into the object reference frame with the object keypoint aligned to the object based on the second position of the object feature in the second optical image; and â¢ wherein driving the set of actuators to move the end effector from the second pose toward the target object comprises driving the set of actuators to move the end effector from the second pose onto the preplanned trajectory and along the preplanned trajectory toward the object keypoint in the object reference frame. 39. A system comprising: â¢ a base; â¢ a robotic arm comprising: o a first beam; o a first joint interposed between first beam and base; o a second beam; and o a second joint interposed between the second beam and the first beam; 

â¢ an end effector transiently coupled to the second beam opposite the second joint and defining an interface surface configured to engage a target object in the vicinity of the base; â¢ an optical sensor coupled to the second beam, defining a field of view extending toward the end effector, and configured to output optical images of the field of view; and â¢ a controller configured to: o actuate the first joint and the second joint to move the end effector from an initial pose to a first pose according to a preplanned trajectory; o identify the target object in a first optical image recorded by the optical sensor when the robotic arm occupies the first pose at a first time; o align the preplanned trajectory to the target object based on a first position of the target object detected in the first optical image; o actuate the first joint and the second joint to move the end effector from the first pose to a second pose along the preplanned trajectory aligned to the target object; o identify the target object in a second optical image recorded by the optical sensor when the robotic arm occupies the second pose at a second time succeeding the first time; o realign the preplanned trajectory to the target object based on a second position of the target object detected in the second optical image; and o actuate the first joint and the second joint to move the end effector from the second pose to a third pose along the preplanned trajectory aligned to the target object. 40. The system of Claim 39: â¢ further comprising: o a first angular position sensor arranged in the first joint; and o a second angular position sensor arranged in the second joint; â¢ wherein the controller is further configured to: o calibrate motion of the first joint relative to outputs of the first angular position sensor and motion of the second joint relative to outputs of the second angular position sensor based on outputs of the first angular position sensor and the second angular position sensor from the first time to the second time and a difference between the first pose and the second pose; 

 o detect absence of the target object in a third optical image recorded by the optical sensor when the robotic arm occupies the third pose at a third time succeeding the second time; and o actuate the first joint and the second joint to move the end effector from the third pose to a fourth pose along the preplanned trajectory according to outputs of the first angular position sensor and the second angular position sensor.
